stories.headline: Trump says Russia and Ukraine agree 3-day ceasefire and prisoner swap
stories.summary: US president says Putin and Zelenskyy agreed to request for cessation beginning May 9

stories.headline: Trump says Russia and Ukraine to observe three-day ceasefire
stories.summary: It comes as the two countries accused each other of violating separate ceasefires to cover the celebrations of the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany.

stories.headline: Oil prices rise after US and Iran exchange fire in Hormuz strait
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says the ceasefire between Washington and Tehran is still in place.

stories.headline: Rubio Meets Meloni as U.S.-Italy Relations Strained and Trump’s Attacks on Pope
stories.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke warmly on Friday of Pope Leo XIV and Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy, after meeting with both leaders after weeks of tensions.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy ‘permits’ Russia to hold its Victory Day parade
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president signed a decree on Friday allowing the Kremlin to hold the Victory Day parade on Red Square in Moscow, but the rest of Russia is not included in the announced ceasefire deal mediated by US President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Trump announces Russia-Ukraine three-day ceasefire from 9 May
stories.summary: US president says on Truth Social the ceasefire will include suspension of all ‘kinetic activity’ and prisoner swapDonald Trump has announced on social media that there will be a three-day ceasefire in the war between Russia and Ukraine from 9 to 11 May.The US president said the ceasefire would include a suspension of all “kinetic activity” and a swap of 1,000 prisoners from each country. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Trump announces three-day ceasefire in Russia-Ukraine war
stories.summary: Truce will also include swap of 1,000 prisoners of war from each country, US president says.

stories.headline: Rubio talks to Meloni and Tajani about US, Italian and EU relations: from the Iran war to Trump's tariffs
stories.summary: The US Secretary of State in talks in Rome with Meloni and Tajani. The Italian minister reiterated his readiness for a mission to Hormuz after the war and Trump's no to tariffs. Rubio promised developments in negotiations with Iran and that "not being able to count" on Allies "is a problem"

stories.headline: Thousands run Palestine Marathon under shadow of Israel’s war on Gaza
stories.summary: Marathon draws 13,000 runners globally to amplify unity and resilience amid escalating violence in Gaza and West Bank.

stories.headline: South Africa president faces call to resign after court ruling
stories.summary: The Constitutional Court says MPs were was wrong to block impeachment proceedings against Cyril Ramaphosa.

stories.headline: Virginia Court Strikes Down Redistricted Voting Map in a Huge Blow to Democrats
stories.summary: The decision is a major victory for Republicans, wiping away a measure approved by voters to allow Democrats to gain as many as four House seats in the midterms.

stories.headline: Virginia Supreme Court strikes down Democrats’ redistricting plan in US
stories.summary: Virginia's top court blocks redistricting plan, handing Republicans a key win before crucial midterm elections.

stories.headline: LNG carriers test Iran blockade in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Pakistan cancelled plans to buy cargoes on spot market because it expected Gulf shipments to resume

stories.headline: US Democrats handed midterm election blow by Virginia court
stories.summary: Referendum approving party’s plan to redraw congressional map is overturned by judges

stories.headline: Pentagon Releases Files on U.F.O.s
stories.summary: The initial files are murky images that show what could be anything. The government said more would be released on a rolling basis.

stories.headline: U.S. Strikes Iranian Targets; Iran Says It Returned Fire
stories.summary: The exchange was the latest twist in a week of mixed signals in the region and tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, as President Trump searches for an off-ramp in the war that he started.

stories.headline: Virginia’s high court strikes down voter-passed House map favoring Democrats
stories.summary: The finding is a major setback for Democrats in their effort to counter GOP-led redistricting in other states. The measure approved April 21 gave Democrats an edge in four districts.

stories.headline: Rubio charms in Rome, but he says Trump’s verbal attacks may continue
stories.summary: The secretary of state held warm meetings with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Pope Leo XIV after the president’s repeated criticism of both leaders.

stories.headline: Libya’s largest oil refinery halts operations during fighting
stories.summary: Zawiya refinery shut down in 'precautionary measure' as emergency declared following explosions and gunfire nearby.

stories.headline: South Africa’s top court revives impeachment inquiry against president
stories.summary: The scandal centres on a large sum of foreign currency stolen from President Cyril Ramaphosa’s farmhouse in 2020.

stories.headline: Three dead after volcano erupts on Indonesian island
stories.summary: Officials had previously issued warnings on climbing the active volcano due to safety concerns.

stories.headline: Pentagon Begins Releasing Wave of Files on UFOs
stories.summary: The Pentagon has begun releasing a wave of government files on unidentified flying objects. Following a directive from President Trump, agencies are now identifying and declassifying decades of records. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Jobs Surge As AI Stocks Wobble | Open Interest 5/8/2026
stories.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Jobs jump, Hormuz tensions rise, and CoreWeave exposes the pressure behind the AI boom. Rick Rieder says stocks aren’t in a bubble, Joanne Hsu explains why consumers are still gloomy, Kevin Hassett rejects Treasury default talk, and top CEOs break down AI, giving, cyber, recycling, and the market rally. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Venezuela’s Faulty Power Grid Risks Derailing Economic Comeback
stories.summary: Venezuela announced emergency measures to stabilize its power grid after electricity consumption hit a nine-year high this week, reviving memories of blackouts and rationing that once crippled the economy.

stories.headline: Trump Reversed Hormuz Plan After Saudis Denied Airspace Access
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia’s refusal of support suggests that President Trump’s unpredictable approach to Iran has strained ties with one of his closest allies in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Oil crisis sparks Japanese dash for corporate credit lines
Editor's Choice: U.S.-China summit tests fragile stability as global tensions rise
Leo XIV, Pope's anniversary in Pompeii and Naples: 'We cannot resign ourselves to death'
stories.summary: The Pontiff is on a pastoral visit to Campania, where he relaunched the call for peace that had marked the first words of his papacy. "The disarming and disarming peace" of a year ago in St Peter's Square became a prayer: "May God enlighten those who have responsibility for government".

stories.headline: Third British national has suspected hantavirus infection, UK government says
stories.summary: Five cases of hantavirus have been confirmed in total, including one of the three passengers on the MV Hondius who died.

stories.headline: World Cup fans in China and India face broadcast uncertainty
stories.summary: Broadcasters in the two countries have yet to reach deals with Fifa, just weeks before the tournament is due to start.

stories.headline: Jane Street earned $10bn in first quarter as it doubled trading revenue
stories.summary: Results cement prop firm as one of the most profitable on Wall Street

stories.headline: Ryan Cohen, GameStop’s rebel CEO
stories.summary: His $56bn hostile bid for eBay has baffled Wall Street, but it could be the world’s first meme stock-driven deal

stories.headline: Markets are banking on the ‘Bliss trade’
stories.summary: A belief in resilience underwritten by big lasting state support is keeping stocks high

stories.headline: Private School Charging $76,000 Offers Discount in Revival Plan
stories.summary: A Colorado private school has tapped the debt markets for $27 million to fund campus upgrades while offering discounts on tuition as part of a turnaround plan.

stories.headline: Blue Owl BDC Won Big New Investment by University of California
stories.summary: A $200 billion investment fund for the University of California system added almost 30 million shares of Blue Owl Technology Finance Corp. in the first quarter, in a sign of institutions seizing upon volatility in the private credit market.

stories.headline: Enhanced Games Owner Jumps 27% After Going Public Via SPAC
stories.summary: Enhanced Group Inc. shares climbed 27% after going public via a merger with blank-check company A Paradise Acquisition Corp. that gave it an enterprise value of $1.2 billion.

stories.headline: Private Credit Going Through 'Growing Pains': Aronov
stories.summary: Oksana Aronov, head of market strategy for alternative fixed income at JPMorgan Asset Management, joins Bloomberg's Caroline Hyde, Emily Graffeo, and Davide Scigliuzzo  on "Bloomberg Real Yield."  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Fed Doesn't Need to Cut Now: Rosner
stories.summary: Lindsay Rosner, head of multi-sector fixed income investing at Goldman Sachs Asset Management, and Oksana Aronov, head of market strategy for alternative fixed income at JPMorgan Asset Management, join Caroline Hyde and Emily Graffeo on "Bloomberg Real Yield."  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Wage Gains Moderated, Jobless Rate Holds |  Real Yield 5/8/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg Real Yield" highlights the market-moving news you need to know. Today's guests: JPMorgan Head of Market Strategy, Alternative Fixed-Income Oksana Aronov, Goldman Sachs Asset Management Lindsay Rosner, and Pimco Multi-Asset Credit Strategist Loti Karoui. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bradesco BBI Sees Brazil IPO Rebound After Compass Debut
stories.summary: The successful initial public offering of Compass Gas e Energia SA, the first in Brazil in nearly five years, means the country’s equity capital markets have now reopened even as global volatility keeps a cap on the pace of new deals, according to Banco Bradesco BBI.

stories.headline: Democratic Gubernatorial Candidate Rob Sand Is Worrying Iowa Republicans
stories.summary: Rob Sand, Iowa’s auditor, is campaigning for governor with a pragmatic message that eschews strict partisanship.

stories.headline: Judge rules DOGE’s cuts to humanities grants were unconstitutional
stories.summary: A federal judge said more than $100 million in cuts to NEH grants were discriminatory, ruling in a case that revealed the inner workings of DOGE.

stories.headline: Woman who returned from Syria refused bail – as it happened

The veteran broadcaster said he expected to celebrate quietly, but expressed gratitude for the well-wishes he had received. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Australian woman accused of joining Islamic State in Syria refused bail
stories.summary: Janai Safar, 32, will remain in custody in Sydney. stories.headline: ‘In the days of old, the world was safe’: West Bank family’s enduring unity
stories.summary: Resistant and resilient, the Masallam family fight to stay on their land in Palestine's occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: Kenya cancer cluster sparks ‘environmental genocide’ claims over oil waste

stories.headline: Cash-strapped Egyptians Zamalek eye record CAF Cup prize money
stories.summary: Zamalek travel to Algeria's USM Alger on Saturday for the first leg of their Confederation Cup final on Saturday.

stories.headline: Honda slips into first operating loss as it reevaluates EV strategy
Toyota forecasts decrease in net profit for FY26 amid Middle East tensions
China's Huayou to buy Africa-focused Australian miner in lithium rush
India's market premium cracks as oil shock meets weak growth
Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
stories.summary: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting because of the impasse in talks with Hamas.

stories.headline: Rosenberg: Russia's Victory Day parade with no tanks a sign Ukraine war not going to plan
stories.summary: For the first time in nearly two decades, there will be no military hardware, just soldiers during Saturday's parade in Moscow.

stories.headline: Iran considering US proposal as Trump says war will be 'over quickly'
stories.summary: Mediator Pakistan says it is "endeavouring to convert this ceasefire into a permanent end to this war".

stories.headline: U.S. intelligence says Iran can outlast Trump’s Hormuz blockade for months
stories.summary: A confidential intelligence community assessment delivered to the White House also finds that Iran retains a substantial missile and drone arsenal.

stories.headline: Aides cite friendly meeting as Rubio aims to mend U.S. ties with Pope Leo
stories.summary: Relations between the United States and the Vatican are at a low point over President Donald Trump’s attacks on Leo, who is a leading critic of the war in Iran.

stories.headline: Anti-war protests rock Japan as PM pushes for stronger defence
stories.summary: Japan's largest anti-war protests in decades highlight a population divided over changes to its pacifist constitution.

stories.headline: Stocks Hit Record High on Jobs as Chipmakers Jump: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Signs of labor-market strength drove stocks to all-time highs, bolstering speculation that the world’s largest economy remains resilient in the face of an energy shock triggered by the Iran war.

stories.headline: Trump pledged to free Jimmy Lai. Now U.S. lawmakers want action at China summit.
stories.summary: The president previously committed to helping the 78-year-old Lai, a symbol of Hong Kong’s pro-democracy movement who has been imprisoned since 2020.

stories.headline: Trump gives EU ultimatum deadline to approve trade deal with US
stories.summary: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

stories.headline: Rwandan singer dies as he was being released from prison
stories.summary: The former university lecturer was a vocal critic of the government and ruling Rwandan Patriotic Front party.

stories.headline: China announces suspended death sentences for former defence ministers
stories.summary: The announcement follows recent ousting of several top officials in China's military.

stories.headline: Disturbances and 127 arrests mar Paris party after PSG Champions League victory
stories.summary: Fans took to the streets to celebrate the Paris team's qualification for the final, but celebrations were marred by disturbances.

stories.headline: Crowds cheer China's new snooker star on return from championship win
stories.summary: Wu Yize, 22, receives a rock star's welcome after becoming second-youngest player to win a world title.

stories.headline: Top BJP leader's aide shot dead in violence after Indian state election
stories.summary: Chandranath Rath was personal assistant to Suvendu Adhikari, front runner to become West Bengal's new chief minister.

stories.headline: DraftKings Profit Beats, Ups Prediction Market Spending
stories.summary: DraftKings Inc. reported revenue that rose 17% to $1.65 billion, slightly ahead of analysts’ estimates for $1.63 billion and said it would spend more on a new push into predictions markets.

stories.headline: Fetterman Says He Has ‘No Plans’ to Leave the Democratic Party
stories.summary: In an opinion piece in The Washington Post, the Pennsylvania senator wrote that while he was “at odds” with the party’s approach to some issues, his values had not changed.

stories.headline: Woman jailed in Somalia for peaceful protest ‘stripped, kicked and beaten’
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview from prison, Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old rickshaw driver, tells of her treatment after being arrested for demonstrating against the governmentA woman being held in prison in Somalia for taking part in peaceful protests has described how she was tortured by her guards.Sadia Moalim Ali, 27, told the Guardian she was stripped naked by two male guards in a room monitored by CCTV, kicked, beaten with a baton and left for

stories.headline: Solomon Islands to get new leader after PM ousted in no-confidence vote
stories.summary: Jeremiah Manele toppled after months of political upheaval in the nation seen as one of China’s closest partners in the Pacific Solomon Islands prime minister Jeremiah Manele lost power in a no-confidence vote in the South Pacific country’s parliament, ending months of political uncertainty.Parliament was adjourned to allow the governor general to make arrangements for the election of a new prime minister. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Russia ignores Ukraine's unilateral ceasefire and attacks kindergarten
stories.summary: Ukraine's president says Russia has "spurned the ceasefire" and Kyiv is deciding on further action to take.

stories.headline: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since Hezbollah ceasefire
stories.summary: Israel says it targeted a senior Hezbollah figure in the capital, the first strike of its kind since mid-April.

stories.headline: Key bridge linking North Korea and Russia almost finished, satellite images show
stories.summary: The road crossing is the latest sign of the deepening relationship between Pyongyang and Moscow.

stories.headline: Only one of Berkshire Hathaway and SoftBank can survive
stories.summary: The two represent competing visions of the future

stories.headline: Sen. Susan Collins discloses ‘essential tremor’ diagnosis to explain shaking
stories.summary: The Maine senator who is up for reelection this year described her condition as ‘extremely common’ with ‘absolutely no impact on my ability to do my job.’

stories.headline: Bowen: Strait of Hormuz standoff raises risk of sliding back into all-out war
stories.summary: The US and Iran's determination to keep the pressure on each other has put the fragile Gulf ceasefire in serious jeopardy.

stories.headline: Nine coal miners die in gas explosion in Colombia
stories.summary: The explosion is the latest accident in the locality of Sutatausa, the site of another deadly mine disaster in 2023.

stories.headline: Some Iranians fear the regime is now more entrenched - and ready for revenge

stories.headline: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
stories.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman are facing the reality of a 10-year prison sentence following their arrest in Iran while on a motorcycle tour last year.

stories.headline: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
stories.summary: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.
